info.title: HeaLink API Documentation
info.description: HeaLink REST API — platform kesehatan mental berbasis AI. Gunakan Bearer token dari endpoint `/api/v1/auth/login` untuk semua endpoint yang memerlukan autentikasi.

post.description:
Creates a new user with role `patient`, initialises an empty profile,
and returns a Sanctum Bearer token ready for immediate use.

Validates email/password credentials. Returns 403 if the account is
inactive. On success, returns the user profile and a Sanctum token.
post.summary: Authenticate and obtain a Bearer token

post.description:
Deletes the current access token. The token will no longer be valid
for subsequent requests.

get.description: Returns the current user object including the nested profile sub-object.

put.description:
All fields are optional. Only the provided fields are updated.
The profile record is created automatically if it doesn't exist yet.

post.description:
Stores the user message and dispatches `AnalyzeChatSentimentJob` which
processes the message asynchronously and stores the AI reply.
post.summary: Send a chat message to the AI

get.description:
Returns a paginated conversation history for the authenticated user
ordered by most recent. Includes both user messages and AI replies.

Returns a paginated list of consultations belonging to the current user,
ordered by most recently scheduled. Optionally filter by status.
get.summary: List consultations for the authenticated patient

get.description:
Returns full details of a consultation. Only the patient who owns the
consultation can access this endpoint.

Returns a paginated list of AI-generated mental health risk assessments,
ordered by most recent. Each entry includes risk level, risk score,
detected emotion, and contributing factors.
get.summary: List mental health risk assessments for the authenticated user

get.description:
Returns the most recent AI-generated mental health risk assessment
for the authenticated user. Returns `null` if no assessment exists yet.
get.summary: Get the latest mental health risk assessment

post.description:
One entry per `journal_date`. Upserts if the date already has a record.
Returns 201 on creation, 200 on update.
post.summary: Create or update a mood journal entry

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of mood journal entries ordered by most recent
date. Supports filtering by exact `date` (YYYY-MM-DD) or by `from`/`to`
date range. `date` takes precedence over the range filter.

Returns a paginated list of in-app notifications ordered by most
recent. The `meta` object includes `unread_count` as a convenience
for badge display.
get.summary: List notifications for the authenticated user

put.description:
Bulk-updates all unread notifications for the authenticated user to
`is_read = true`. Returns 200 with no data payload.

Marks the notification identified by its UUID as read. Returns 403
if the notification does not belong to the authenticated user.
put.summary: Mark a single notification as read

put.description:
Each user has a single health screening record that is upserted on every
call. BMI is automatically computed from `height_cm` and `weight_kg`.
PHQ-9 total score is auto-calculated when `phq9_answers` is provided
(array of 9 integers 0–3). Returns 201 on first creation, 200 on update.
put.summary: Create or update the health screening record

get.description:
Returns the most-recently updated health screening for the authenticated
user. Returns `null` if no screening has been submitted yet.
get.summary: Get the latest health screening record

post.description:
Creates a new sleep log for the given `sleep_date`, or updates the
existing one if the date already has a record. Returns 201 on creation
and 200 on update.
post.summary: Store or update a sleep log entry

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of sleep logs ordered by most recent date.
Optionally filter by date range using `from` and `to` (YYYY-MM-DD).

post.description:
Stores a wearable reading (HRV, heart rate, stress index) and
dispatches the `VitalDataSynced` event which triggers the AI risk
assessment pipeline.

get.description:
Returns the single most-recent `WearableData` record for the
authenticated user, ordered by `recorded_at`. Returns `null` if none
exists yet.

Returns a paginated history of wearable readings ordered by most
recent. Optionally filter by date range using `from` and `to`
(ISO-8601 datetime strings).

errors.description: A detailed description of each field that failed validation.
